home.benefit.remote.title.message: Jump back into the current work without guessing

home.benefit.remote.body.message: Open the work that is already in progress on the Mac from the list so it takes less time to recover where you should look first.

home.benefit.setup.title.message: Connect quickly with QR on the same LAN

home.benefit.setup.body.message: Open the Mac app, show the QR, and scan it from the iPhone. Adding the Mac finishes in a few steps.

home.benefit.context.body.message: Conversations, branches, skills, and commands stay grouped around each work item so it is easier to keep track of what environment you are touching.

home.step.bridge.body.message: Prepare the QR code and the work list in the Mac app.

home.step.pair.body.message: Import with QR or a code, then wait for connection checks and the work list to load.

home.step.resume.title.message: Open the work and reply from there

home.step.resume.body.message: Open the work, follow the conversation, add a quick note from the input box, or start a new conversation when you need it.

home.scenario.commute.title.message: You want to check progress from the couch

home.scenario.commute.body.message: Leave the longer task on the Mac and just open the conversation on iPhone to see how it is going.

home.guide.iphone.body.message: The entry point for people who want adding a Mac, browsing work, reading conversations, and writing from the input box explained first.

home.guide.mac.body.message: The entry point for people who want the Mac app, connection layer, QR, and logs explained first.

home.guide.shared.body.message: A dedicated section for topics that cross the boundary between iPhone and Mac.

home.guide.reference.body.message: The place for cross-cutting information such as release policy and multilingual support.

home.meta.title.message: Resume Codex on your Mac from your iPhone

home.meta.description.message: CodexPocket is an iPhone app that helps you resume the Codex running on your Mac without going back to your desk.

home.hero.title.message: Resume the Codex running on your Mac from your iPhone.

home.hero.lead.message: Do not break the flow just because you stepped away from your desk. CodexPocket keeps the Codex environment on the Mac and lets you open work, check the current state, and add a quick follow-up from your iPhone, so you can clear short checks before you return to the Mac.

home.hero.meta.public.message: Mac version {stableVersion} is available now. Once the iPhone app is installed, you can finish the first connection by scanning a QR code on the same LAN.

home.hero.meta.unavailable.message: Mac version {stableVersion} is available now. The iPhone side does not yet have a public App Store / TestFlight link, so check the current distribution status first.

home.hero.note.newThread.message: Execution and logs stay on the Mac

home.hero.note.sync.message: Use iPhone for checks and light follow-ups

home.visual.title.message: Control on iPhone, execution on Mac. That makes it easier to fit into the spaces between other things.

home.visual.body.message: The Mac app and connection layer keep the working state on the Mac, while the iPhone becomes the quick way back in when you need it.

home.visual.action.value.message: Start a new conversation from the input box

home.visual.panelTitle.message: What you can touch on iPhone

home.visual.panel.project.body.message: The entry point for conversations and branches

home.visual.panel.composer.body.message: The entry point for quick notes, skills, and one-off execution

home.limitations.lan.body.message: The main goal is a quick resume when the Mac and iPhone are on the same LAN.

home.limitations.realtime.title.message: Execution and source of truth stay on the Mac

home.limitations.realtime.body.message: Connection handling, logs, and work management stay on the Mac, while the iPhone focuses on checking and input.

home.limitations.future.title.message: Not built for always-on from-anywhere access

home.limitations.future.body.message: It fits best when you want to make short interactions easier after stepping away, not when you want a permanent remote setup from anywhere.

home.section.benefits.title.message: Cut the cost of interruption after you leave your desk

home.section.limits.title.message: It lands best when these assumptions match

home.section.scenarios.title.message: These are the moments where it clicks
